top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
top_strategic.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, wristband. Near-zero: sunglasses(2). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
top_strategic.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

top_strategic.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 65 players with 5+ clips
top_strategic.evidence: 65 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(661), Chintan(186), Tmf(133), Coach_Block(117), Ironvarr(107)
top_strategic.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace detected in 4185 clips (97.0%) — sell to brands
top_strategic.evidence: We detect unbranded equipment/space in 4185 clips. This is the gap between what's visible and what's branded — sellable data.
top_strategic.action: Package sponsorship whitespace data as 'brand opportunity report' for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Endorsement intelligence: 179 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(1087x), Chintan-JOOLA(293x), Tmf-JOOLA(285x), Ironvarr-JOOLA(226x), Coach_Block-JOOLA(221x)
top_strategic.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Social caption generation at 99.9% — ready to productize
top_strategic.evidence: Social media captions are generated for 4310/4313 clips. This is already a viable content-as-a-service product.
top_strategic.action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

top_data.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.12 viral vs 'none' at 1
top_data.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.12, speed_up=4.27, reset=3.88, volley=3.51, dink=2.92, drive=2.87, serve=2.18, unknown=1.14, none=1
top_data.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.99 (precision=0.99, recall=0.98)
top_data.evidence: Across 524 verified clips: 2065 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.986.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 4283 clips with signatures avg 2.9 viral vs 3.9
top_data.evidence: 4283 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 2.9 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
top_data.action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

top_data.title: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
top_data.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (554/6973 are venue), Nicol (6/14 are venue), PUSHIT (5/9 are venue), Onix (4/45 are venue), adidas (4/47 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=6419, Nike=685, PICKLEBALL=235, Reolink=132, HEAD=123.
top_data.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

all_findings.title: Viral formula: quality (7.39 vs 4.37) + watchability (6.78 vs 2.43)
all_findings.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.39, watchability=6.78. Bottom 100: quality=4.37, watchability=2.43. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
all_findings.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Worst predicted badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.964), hustler (F1=0.93)
all_findings.evidence: Lowest F1 badges: third shot drop artist (F1=0.964), hustler (F1=0.93). These need prompt improvement.
all_findings.action: Add specific examples for these badge types in Gemini prompt

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.1 vs short=2.2)
all_findings.evidence: Avg rally: 15.6 shots. Long rallies (15+): 2193 clips, avg viral 3.1. Short (4-): 66 clips, avg viral 2.2.
all_findings.action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 374/524 verified clips — add disambiguation
all_findings.evidence: JOOLA dominates 71% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
all_findings.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

all_findings.action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'athletic' is 220.0x more likely in top clips (22.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'athletic' appears in 22.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'athletic' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Brand-skill split: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM users excel at kitchen (7.5), PSA users at power (6.29)
all_findings.evidence: Among brands with 10+ clips: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.5), while PSA has highest power rating (6.29).
all_findings.action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

all_findings.title: Skill standout: Ajr averages 8.5 in consistency across 6 clips

all_findings.title: 'dink' players skew kitchen: power=3.6 vs kitchen=7.2
all_findings.evidence: Players using dink as dominant shot (2684 clips) average power=3.6 and kitchen=7.2.
all_findings.action: Use shot type as player skill segmentation signal

all_findings.title: Best predicted badges: switch play (F1=1.0), one-handed backhand (F1=1.0), fist pump (F1=1.0)
all_findings.evidence: Top performing badges by F1 score: switch play (F1=1.0), one-handed backhand (F1=1.0), fist pump (F1=1.0)
all_findings.action: Use these badges as confidence anchors for the prediction system

connections.strategic_finding: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection

product_concept.name: BrandGap Filler
product_concept.first_screen: Dashboard showing 4 equipment categories (shoes, rackets, apparel, bags) with sponsorship void indicators. User selects a category → sees 5 missed badge examples from training data with side-by-side 'before detection' and 'after detection' annotations, then a 'Generate Sponsorship Pitch' button populated with untapped market size.
product_concept.why_powerful: Converts unspotted sponsorship whitespace into immediate pitch decks by fixing the detection blindness that's leaving money on the table.

connections.strategic_finding: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
connections.data_finding: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
connections.combined_insight: Combine 'Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment cate' with 'Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — t' for a targeted product.

product_concept.name: RealShare Court Analytics
product_concept.first_screen: Split-screen view: left side shows raw brand detection counts (inflated), right side shows 'corrected share-of-court' after filtering out net/venue branding noise. User toggles between 7 affected brands to see true vs. reported visibility %, then exports corrected sponsorship valuation report.
product_concept.why_powerful: Exposes the gap between what sponsors think they're getting and actual court presence, enabling accurate sponsorship pricing and realistic brand lift forecasts.

product_concept.name: PlayerClip Amplifier
product_concept.first_screen: List of 65 eligible players (5+ clips minimum) ranked by predicted viral score. User selects a player → sees their clip library with quality score (7.39 threshold highlight) and watchability index (6.78 benchmark), then one-click 'Create Viral Cut' that auto-generates highlight reel matching the formula.
product_concept.why_powerful: Systematically converts latent longitudinal player data into reproducible viral content by applying tested quality and watchability formulas instead of guessing.
